name: Organization Context Ontology
description: Rich organizational context hub linking products, brands, competitive landscape, market segments, and maturity levels. Foundation layer bridge connecting ORG entities to domain-specific ontologies via the context hub pattern.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Organization Context
hasDefinedTerm.description: Rich context container for an organization linking to all contextual sub-entities including products, brands, competitive landscape, market context, and maturity assessment
hasDefinedTerm.skos:note: Central hub entity — all other ORG-CONTEXT entities connect through this

org-ctx:properties.name: Context ID
org-ctx:properties.description: Unique identifier for this organizational context instance

org-ctx:properties.name: Organization Reference
org-ctx:properties.description: Reference to the core organization entity in the ORG ontology

org-ctx:properties.name: Products
org-ctx:properties.description: Organization's products or services offered to market

org-ctx:properties.name: Brands
org-ctx:properties.description: Organization's brand portfolio

org-ctx:properties.name: Value Proposition References
org-ctx:properties.description: References to value propositions defined in VSOM ontology — details live there, not here

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Portfolio
org-ctx:properties.description: Full product or service portfolio structure

org-ctx:properties.name: Competitive Landscape
org-ctx:properties.description: Competitive environment context for the organization

org-ctx:properties.name: Market Context
org-ctx:properties.description: Market segments, geography, and localization context

org-ctx:properties.name: Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Organization maturity assessment across multiple dimensions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Product
hasDefinedTerm.description: Product or service offered by the organization to its target market segments

org-ctx:properties.name: Product ID
org-ctx:properties.description: Unique identifier for this product

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Name
org-ctx:properties.description: Display name of the product or service

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Description
org-ctx:properties.description: Brief description of the product's purpose and offering

org-ctx:properties.name: Category
org-ctx:properties.description: Product category classification

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Segment
org-ctx:properties.description: Product segment within the portfolio

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Status
org-ctx:properties.description: Current lifecycle status: active, discontinued, or planned

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Value Proposition
org-ctx:properties.description: Reference to product-level value proposition in VSOM ontology if different from brand-level

hasDefinedTerm.name: Brand
hasDefinedTerm.description: Brand owned by the organization, carrying its own market identity and value proposition

org-ctx:properties.name: Brand ID
org-ctx:properties.description: Unique identifier for this brand

org-ctx:properties.name: Brand Name
org-ctx:properties.description: Display name of the brand

org-ctx:properties.name: Brand Description
org-ctx:properties.description: Brief description of the brand's identity and positioning

org-ctx:properties.name: Is Primary Brand
org-ctx:properties.description: Whether this is the organization's primary brand

org-ctx:properties.name: Brand Value Proposition
org-ctx:properties.description: Reference to brand-level value proposition in VSOM ontology

hasDefinedTerm.name: Product Portfolio
hasDefinedTerm.description: Structured view of the organization's complete product and service offerings across segments

org-ctx:properties.name: Portfolio Type
org-ctx:properties.description: Classification of portfolio structure: single_product, multi_product, ecommerce, service, or hybrid

org-ctx:properties.name: Product Count
org-ctx:properties.description: Approximate number of products in the portfolio

org-ctx:properties.name: Portfolio Segments
org-ctx:properties.description: Product segments within the portfolio

hasDefinedTerm.name: Competitive Landscape
hasDefinedTerm.description: Competitive environment context encompassing named competitors, Porter-style forces, and macro trends affecting the organization's market position
hasDefinedTerm.skos:note: Competitors can exist at multiple levels: platform, client org, client's client

org-ctx:properties.name: Competitors
org-ctx:properties.description: Named competitors with strengths, weaknesses, and strategic posture analysis

org-ctx:properties.name: Competitive Forces
org-ctx:properties.description: Porter-style forces: threat of entry, substitutes, buyer power, supplier power, rivalry

org-ctx:properties.name: Trends
org-ctx:properties.description: Macro trends affecting the competitive landscape: regulatory, technological, economic, social

hasDefinedTerm.name: Competitor
hasDefinedTerm.description: Named competitor organization with competitive analysis including type, priority, strengths, weaknesses, and strategic posture

org-ctx:properties.name: Organization Reference
org-ctx:properties.description: Reference to the competitor's entry in the ORG ontology

org-ctx:properties.name: Competitor Type
org-ctx:properties.description: Classification: direct, indirect, aspirational, or substitute

org-ctx:properties.name: Priority Ranking
org-ctx:properties.description: Threat priority ranking from 1 (highest) to 5 (lowest)

org-ctx:properties.name: Strengths
org-ctx:properties.description: Competitor's key competitive strengths

org-ctx:properties.name: Weaknesses
org-ctx:properties.description: Competitor's key competitive weaknesses

org-ctx:properties.name: Strategic Posture
org-ctx:properties.description: Current strategic stance: aggressive, defensive, expanding, contracting, or stable

org-ctx:properties.name: Track Citations
org-ctx:properties.description: Whether to track AI platform citations for this competitor

org-ctx:properties.name: Track Rankings
org-ctx:properties.description: Whether to track search rankings for this competitor

hasDefinedTerm.name: Competitive Force
hasDefinedTerm.description: Porter-style competitive force analysing structural market pressure including threat of entry, substitutes, buyer and supplier power, and competitive rivalry

org-ctx:properties.name: Force Type
org-ctx:properties.description: Porter force classification: threat_of_new_entrants, threat_of_substitutes, bargaining_power_buyers, bargaining_power_suppliers, competitive_rivalry

org-ctx:properties.name: Intensity
org-ctx:properties.description: Force intensity level: low, medium, or high

org-ctx:properties.name: Description
org-ctx:properties.description: Narrative description of this competitive force's impact

org-ctx:properties.name: Key Factors
org-ctx:properties.description: Specific factors driving this competitive force

hasDefinedTerm.name: Trend
hasDefinedTerm.description: Macro trend affecting the market landscape including regulatory, technological, economic, social, environmental, and political forces

org-ctx:properties.name: Trend Type
org-ctx:properties.description: PESTEL classification: regulatory, technological, economic, social, environmental, political

org-ctx:properties.name: Trend Name
org-ctx:properties.description: Descriptive name for the macro trend

org-ctx:properties.name: Trend Description
org-ctx:properties.description: Narrative description of the trend and its implications

org-ctx:properties.name: Impact
org-ctx:properties.description: Direction of impact: positive, negative, neutral, or uncertain

org-ctx:properties.name: Timeframe
org-ctx:properties.description: Expected impact horizon: immediate, short_term, medium_term, long_term

hasDefinedTerm.name: Market Context
hasDefinedTerm.description: Market segments, geographic scope, customer needs, and localization context defining the organization's addressable market

org-ctx:properties.name: Market Segments
org-ctx:properties.description: Target industries, geographies, and customer segments

org-ctx:properties.name: Customer Needs
org-ctx:properties.description: High-level jobs-to-be-done and pain points — detailed analysis lives in VSOM ontology

org-ctx:properties.name: Geographic Scope
org-ctx:properties.description: Operating regions and geographic markets served

org-ctx:properties.name: Localization
org-ctx:properties.description: Localization requirements including base currency, geography, and supported languages

hasDefinedTerm.name: Market Segment
hasDefinedTerm.description: Target market segment defined by industry, geography, size, and strategic priority for the organization

org-ctx:properties.name: Segment ID
org-ctx:properties.description: Unique identifier for this market segment

org-ctx:properties.name: Segment Name
org-ctx:properties.description: Descriptive name for the market segment

org-ctx:properties.name: Industry
org-ctx:properties.description: Industry vertical this segment belongs to

org-ctx:properties.name: Geography
org-ctx:properties.description: Geographic region for this segment

org-ctx:properties.name: Segment Size
org-ctx:properties.description: Estimated addressable market size for this segment

org-ctx:properties.name: Priority
org-ctx:properties.description: Strategic priority: primary, secondary, or emerging

hasDefinedTerm.name: Customer Need
hasDefinedTerm.description: High-level customer need identifier for jobs-to-be-done, pain points, and value drivers — detailed pains and gains analysis lives in VSOM ontology

org-ctx:properties.name: Need ID
org-ctx:properties.description: Unique identifier for this customer need

org-ctx:properties.name: Need Type
org-ctx:properties.description: Classification: job_to_be_done, pain_point, value_driver, or unmet_need

org-ctx:properties.name: Need Description
org-ctx:properties.description: Description of the customer need and its business impact

org-ctx:properties.name: Priority
org-ctx:properties.description: Urgency and importance: critical, high, medium, or low

hasDefinedTerm.name: Organization Maturity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Multi-dimensional maturity assessment covering size, value delivery, marketing, technology, complexity management, and AI readiness

org-ctx:properties.name: Size Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Size maturity level (1-5) based on financial scale and headcount

org-ctx:properties.name: Value Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Value delivery maturity level (1-5)

org-ctx:properties.name: Marketing Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Marketing capability maturity level (1-5) including team structure and functional roles

org-ctx:properties.name: Technology Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Technology and digital maturity level (1-5)

org-ctx:properties.name: Complexity Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: Complexity management maturity level (1-5)

org-ctx:properties.name: AI Maturity
org-ctx:properties.description: AI-specific maturity assessment: unaware, exploring, experimenting, implementing, optimizing

hasDefinedTerm.name: CompetitorProfile
hasDefinedTerm.description: Detailed profile of a competitor within the landscape, capturing market position, strengths, vulnerabilities, and strategic intent. Absorbed from CL-ONT v2.0.0.
hasDefinedTerm.skos:note: Extends Competitor with analytical depth — market share estimates, key differentiators, and threat assessment

additionalProperty.name: Profile ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this competitor profile

additionalProperty.name: Market Position
additionalProperty.description: Current market position: leader, challenger, follower, nicher

additionalProperty.name: Estimated Market Share
additionalProperty.description: Estimated market share percentage (0-100)

additionalProperty.name: Strategic Intent
additionalProperty.description: Observed strategic direction: expand, defend, harvest, divest

additionalProperty.name: Key Differentiators
additionalProperty.description: Primary competitive differentiators (array of strings)

additionalProperty.name: Vulnerabilities
additionalProperty.description: Known competitor vulnerabilities or exploitable weaknesses

hasDefinedTerm.name: MarketDynamic
hasDefinedTerm.description: A significant trend, force, or dynamic shaping the competitive landscape. Complements Trend with competitive-specific dynamics. Absorbed from CL-ONT v2.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Dynamic ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this market dynamic

additionalProperty.name: Dynamic Type
additionalProperty.description: Type: Technology-Shift, Regulatory-Change, Consumer-Behavior, Economic-Factor, Competitive-Move, Supply-Chain, ESG-Pressure

additionalProperty.name: Impact Level
additionalProperty.description: Impact on landscape: low, medium, high, transformative

additionalProperty.name: Time Horizon
additionalProperty.description: Expected time horizon: immediate, short-term, medium-term, long-term

additionalProperty.name: Strategic Implication
additionalProperty.description: Interpretation of the dynamic's strategic significance

hasDefinedTerm.name: CompetitiveAdvantage
hasDefinedTerm.description: A specific competitive advantage held or sought by the organization within the landscape. Absorbed from CL-ONT v2.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Advantage ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this competitive advantage

additionalProperty.name: Advantage Type
additionalProperty.description: Type: cost-leadership, differentiation, focus, innovation, network-effect, brand-equity

additionalProperty.name: Sustainability Level
additionalProperty.description: How sustainable this advantage is: temporary, durable, structural

additionalProperty.name: Imitability Risk
additionalProperty.description: Risk of competitors replicating this advantage: low, medium, high

additionalProperty.name: Current Status
additionalProperty.description: Status: emerging, established, declining, lost

hasDefinedTerm.name: StrategicOpportunity
hasDefinedTerm.description: An identified opportunity within the competitive landscape, including blue ocean opportunities. Absorbed from CL-ONT v2.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Opportunity ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this strategic opportunity

additionalProperty.name: Opportunity Type
additionalProperty.description: Type: market-expansion, product-innovation, acquisition, partnership, blue-ocean, digital-transformation

additionalProperty.name: Estimated Value
additionalProperty.description: Estimated commercial value or revenue impact

additionalProperty.name: Competitive Barriers
additionalProperty.description: Barriers to capturing this opportunity

additionalProperty.name: Fit Score
additionalProperty.description: Alignment with organizational capabilities (1-5)

additionalProperty.name: Risk Level
additionalProperty.description: Risk assessment: low, medium, high, very-high

hasDefinedTerm.name: CompetitiveAnalysis
hasDefinedTerm.description: Analytical report or assessment of competitive landscape. Links competitors to structured analysis with findings and recommendations. Absorbed from CA-ONT v3.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Analysis ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this competitive analysis

additionalProperty.name: Analysis Type
additionalProperty.description: Type: swot, porters-five-forces, value-chain, benchmarking, strategic-group, blue-ocean

additionalProperty.name: Analysis Date
additionalProperty.description: Date the analysis was conducted (ISO 8601)

additionalProperty.name: Key Findings
additionalProperty.description: Summary of key findings from the analysis (array of strings)

additionalProperty.name: Recommendations
additionalProperty.description: Strategic recommendations derived from the analysis (array of strings)

additionalProperty.name: Confidence Level
additionalProperty.description: Confidence in findings: low, medium, high, very-high

hasDefinedTerm.name: CompetitorAssessment
hasDefinedTerm.description: Assessment of a specific competitor within a competitive analysis. Links a Competitor entity to analytical results. Absorbed from CA-ONT v3.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Assessment ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this competitor assessment

additionalProperty.name: Threat Level
additionalProperty.description: Assessed threat level: minimal, low, moderate, high, critical

additionalProperty.name: Domain Capabilities
additionalProperty.description: Competitor's domain-specific capabilities and strengths (array of strings)

hasDefinedTerm.name: MarketOffering
hasDefinedTerm.description: Products and services offered in the market by competitors or the target organization. Complements Product with competitive positioning. Absorbed from CA-ONT v3.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Offering ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this market offering

additionalProperty.name: Offering Category
additionalProperty.description: Category: SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, Professional-Services, Managed-Services, Consulting, Hardware, Hybrid

additionalProperty.name: Target Audience
additionalProperty.description: Primary audience: enterprise, mid-market, SMB, consumer, developer, government

additionalProperty.name: Pricing Model
additionalProperty.description: Pricing approach: subscription, consumption, perpetual, freemium, tiered, custom

additionalProperty.name: Unique Value Proposition
additionalProperty.description: What differentiates this offering from competitors

hasDefinedTerm.name: CompetitivePosition
hasDefinedTerm.description: An organization's position relative to competitors in the market. Tracks leadership status, brand strength, and innovation capability. Absorbed from CA-ONT v3.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Position ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this competitive position

additionalProperty.name: Market Leadership Status
additionalProperty.description: Status: market-leader, strong-challenger, established-player, emerging-player, niche-specialist

additionalProperty.name: Brand Strength
additionalProperty.description: Brand strength assessment: weak, developing, established, strong, dominant

additionalProperty.name: Innovation Capability
additionalProperty.description: Innovation capability level: laggard, follower, fast-follower, innovator, pioneer

hasDefinedTerm.name: BlueOceanOpportunity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Market opportunities with minimal direct competition — uncontested market spaces. Absorbed from CA-ONT v3.0.0.

additionalProperty.name: Blue Ocean ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this blue ocean opportunity

additionalProperty.name: Market Size
additionalProperty.description: Estimated addressable market size

additionalProperty.name: Accessibility Level
additionalProperty.description: How accessible the opportunity is given current capabilities: low, medium, high

additionalProperty.name: Strategic Value
additionalProperty.description: Overall strategic value assessment: low, medium, high, transformative

hasDefinedTerm.name: AuthorityDomain
hasDefinedTerm.description: Classification of a domain authority's area of expertise and jurisdiction. Links organizations of type StandardsBody, GovernmentAgency, or InternationalOrg to their governance, regulatory, or standards domain. Added v3.1.0 for CG-ONT domain authority modelling.
hasDefinedTerm.skos:note: Domain authorities publish governance codes, standards, and guides that are modelled in CG-ONT. This entity classifies what domain each authority operates in.

additionalProperty.name: Authority Domain ID
additionalProperty.description: Unique identifier for this authority domain classification

additionalProperty.name: Domain Name
additionalProperty.description: Name of the domain: CyberSecurity, DataProtection, RiskManagement, InformationSecurity, CloudSecurity, CorporateGovernance, ITGovernance, FinancialRegulation, GlobalRisk

additionalProperty.name: Domain Description
additionalProperty.description: Description of the authority's domain of expertise and influence

additionalProperty.name: Jurisdiction Scope
additionalProperty.description: Geographic scope of authority: Global, Regional, National, Sub-National

additionalProperty.name: Authority Type
additionalProperty.description: Classification of authority: NationalAgency, StandardsBody, IndustryBody, Regulator, InternationalOrg, ProfessionalAssociation

additionalProperty.name: Authority Organization Reference
additionalProperty.description: Reference to the authority's entry in the ORG ontology (org:Organization with type StandardsBody/GovernmentAgency/InternationalOrg)

relationships.name: hasProducts
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext contains the organization's products and services

relationships.name: hasBrands
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext contains the organization's brand portfolio

relationships.name: hasPortfolio
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has one ProductPortfolio structuring its offerings

relationships.name: hasCompetitiveLandscape
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has one CompetitiveLandscape defining its competitive environment

relationships.name: hasMarketContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has one MarketContext defining its addressable market

relationships.name: hasMaturity
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has one maturity assessment across all dimensions

relationships.name: brandHasProduct
relationships.description: A Brand encompasses one or more Products in its market identity

relationships.name: productInPortfolio
relationships.description: A Product belongs to the organization's ProductPortfolio

relationships.name: hasCompetitors
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape contains named Competitor entries for analysis

relationships.name: hasForces
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape contains Porter-style CompetitiveForce analyses

relationships.name: hasTrends
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape contains macro Trend observations

relationships.name: hasSegments
relationships.description: A MarketContext contains target MarketSegments

relationships.name: hasCustomerNeeds
relationships.description: A MarketContext identifies high-level CustomerNeeds across all segments

relationships.name: segmentHasNeed
relationships.description: A MarketSegment has specific CustomerNeeds driving demand in that segment

relationships.name: segmentShapedBy
relationships.description: A MarketSegment is shaped by CompetitiveForces that constrain or enable opportunity

relationships.name: competitorTargets
relationships.description: A Competitor targets one or more MarketSegments in its strategy

relationships.name: trendImpacts
relationships.description: A Trend impacts one or more MarketSegments, altering competitive dynamics

relationships.name: hasMarketDynamics
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape tracks MarketDynamics — shifts, disruptions, and emerging patterns in the market

relationships.name: hasCompetitiveAdvantages
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape identifies CompetitiveAdvantages held by the organization or its competitors

relationships.name: hasStrategicOpportunities
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape reveals StrategicOpportunities arising from market gaps and competitive positioning

relationships.name: hasCompetitiveAnalyses
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape contains formal CompetitiveAnalysis records (SWOT, Porter's, benchmarking, etc.)

relationships.name: hasCompetitivePosition
relationships.description: A CompetitiveLandscape has a CompetitivePosition summarising the organization's market standing

relationships.name: hasCompetitorProfile
relationships.description: A Competitor has a CompetitorProfile providing detailed competitive intelligence

relationships.name: hasCompetitorAssessments
relationships.description: A CompetitiveAnalysis includes CompetitorAssessments evaluating individual competitors

relationships.name: competitorHasOfferings
relationships.description: A Competitor offers MarketOfferings — products and services competing in the same market space

relationships.name: contextForOrganization
relationships.description: An ORG Organization has a rich context defined in this ontology via the bridge pattern

relationships.name: hasValueProposition
relationships.description: OrganizationContext references value propositions defined in the VSOM ontology

relationships.name: brandValueProposition
relationships.description: A Brand has a value proposition defined in the VSOM ontology

relationships.name: productValueProposition
relationships.description: A Product may have a product-level value proposition in the VSOM ontology

relationships.name: hasContextAssignments
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has context type assignments linking it to typed context dimensions from the ContextTypes ontology

relationships.name: maturityClassifiedByFunction
relationships.description: An OrganizationMaturity maps to per-function FunctionalMaturity assessments in ContextTypes for marketing, sales, operations, and other business functions

relationships.name: maturityClassifiedByTech
relationships.description: The technology maturity dimension of OrganizationMaturity maps to TechnologyMaturity in ContextTypes for cloud, digital transformation, and automation assessment

relationships.name: maturityClassifiedByAI
relationships.description: The AI maturity dimension of OrganizationMaturity maps to AIReadiness in ContextTypes for data readiness, talent, and deployment assessment

relationships.name: sizedByContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext maps to OrganisationSize in ContextTypes providing multi-dimensional size classification via headcount, customers, revenue, and KPI-linked metrics

relationships.name: marketClassifiedBy
relationships.description: A MarketContext is classified by MarketClassification entries from ContextTypes using standard industry schemes (SIC, NAICS, GICS) with sub-sector hierarchy

relationships.name: segmentTypedBy
relationships.description: A MarketSegment in ORG-CONTEXT is typed by a MarketSegment classification in ContextTypes providing segmentation taxonomy, addressable market sizing, and growth metrics

relationships.name: hasCultureContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has a CultureContext from ContextTypes capturing innovation orientation, risk tolerance, collaboration style, and decision-making patterns

relationships.name: hasGeoContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has a GeoContext from ContextTypes capturing headquarters location, operating regions, market reach, and regulatory jurisdictions

relationships.name: hasMarketPositioning
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has MarketPositioning from ContextTypes capturing competitive position, market share, brand strength, and differentiation strategy

relationships.name: competitorBridgedByContext
relationships.description: A Competitor in ORG-CONTEXT is bridged to CompetitorContext in ContextTypes for threat assessment, recommended responses, and context-aware watch-list management

relationships.name: hasRCSGContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has an RCSGContext from RCSG-FW defining which regulatory, compliance, security, and governance requirements apply to the organisation

relationships.name: hasLocalizationContext
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has LocalizationContext from ContextTypes providing currency, tax jurisdiction, data residency, and content localization variables per operating region

relationships.name: brandHasDesignSystem
relationships.description: A Brand has one or more Design Systems governing its visual identity, token cascades, and Figma source bindings

relationships.name: hasAuthorityDomains
relationships.description: An OrganizationContext has AuthorityDomain classifications linking the organization to its governance, regulatory, or standards domains. Applicable to organizations of type StandardsBody, GovernmentAgency, or InternationalOrg.

businessRules.name: ContextRequiresOrganization
businessRules.description: Every OrganizationContext must reference a valid organization from the ORG ontology

businessRules.name: CompetitorLimit
businessRules.description: A CompetitiveLandscape should have no more than 10 tracked competitors for focus

businessRules.name: MaturityScoresRange
businessRules.description: All maturity dimension scores must be integers between 1 and 5
businessRules.action: THEN all maturity levels BETWEEN 1 AND 5

businessRules.name: ValuePropositionReferenceOnly
businessRules.description: OrganizationContext only references value propositions — detailed pains, gains, solution, and ICP data lives in the VSOM ontology
businessRules.action: THEN vsom:ValueProposition reference IS valid AND NOT inline definition

businessRules.name: BrandMustBeNamed
businessRules.description: Every Brand must have a name for identification and market recognition

businessRules.name: SegmentShouldHaveNeeds
businessRules.description: Market segments should identify at least one customer need to justify targeting

crossOntologyReferences.description: ORG-CONTEXT is a Foundation layer hub bridging ORG entities to domain-specific ontologies. It receives organizational identity from ORG, references value propositions in VSOM, and connects to ContextTypes for extensible multi-dimensional context classification spanning market, size, maturity (functional/tech/IT/AI), culture, geography, positioning, competitor, RCSG (regulatory/compliance/security/governance), and localization dimensions. Competitive analysis entities align with CA and CL ontologies.

lineageAlignment.org:Organization: ORG provides the core organization entity that ORG-CONTEXT enriches with market context
lineageAlignment.vsom:ValueProposition: VSOM holds detailed value proposition definitions referenced by ORG-CONTEXT brands and products
lineageAlignment.ca:CompetitiveAnalysis: CA provides competitive analysis frameworks that complement ORG-CONTEXT competitor tracking
lineageAlignment.cl:CompetitiveLandscape: CL provides landscape models that ORG-CONTEXT competitive data feeds into
lineageAlignment.ctx:ContextType: ContextTypes provides an extensible taxonomy of context dimensions assignable to organisations via ContextAssignment
lineageAlignment.ctx:MarketClassification: ContextTypes MarketClassification provides industry and market classification with hierarchical sub-sector support
lineageAlignment.ctx:OrganisationSize: ContextTypes OrganisationSize provides multi-dimensional size metrics (headcount, customers, revenue, KPI-linked)
lineageAlignment.ctx:FunctionalMaturity: ContextTypes FunctionalMaturity provides per-function maturity assessment (marketing, sales, ops, etc.)
lineageAlignment.ctx:TechnologyMaturity: ContextTypes TechnologyMaturity provides cloud, digital transformation, and automation maturity with IT and AI sub-types
lineageAlignment.ctx:CultureContext: ContextTypes CultureContext provides innovation orientation, risk tolerance, collaboration, and decision-making culture dimensions
lineageAlignment.ctx:GeoContext: ContextTypes GeoContext provides HQ location, operating regions, market reach, and regulatory jurisdictions
lineageAlignment.ctx:MarketPositioning: ContextTypes MarketPositioning provides competitive position, market share, brand strength, and extensible positioning criteria
lineageAlignment.ctx:CompetitorContext: ContextTypes CompetitorContext bridges to competitor analysis with threat assessment and strategic recommendations
lineageAlignment.rcsg-fw:RCSGContext: RCSG-FW RCSGContext defines which regulatory, compliance, security, and governance requirements apply to the organisation — bridges to RCSG-Series implementation ontologies (GDPR, PII, MCSB)
lineageAlignment.ctx:LocalizationContext: ContextTypes LocalizationContext provides currency, tax jurisdiction, data residency, and content localization variables per operating region
lineageAlignment.ds:DesignSystem: DS-ONT DesignSystem provides the brand's visual identity system including token cascades, Figma source bindings, and theme mode support
